outlook.headline: 2.5D packaging demand broadens as AI keeps supply tight
outlook.summary: The read-through is still getting better: most companies citing 2.5D/advanced packaging point to hot AI-driven demand and tight capacity, with TSMC, Amkor, Onto, Veeco, KLA and others all calling out stronger demand or higher revenue/content. Beneficiaries are the suppliers with direct exposure to constrained advanced-packaging capacity and AI/HBM ramps, while neutral names are simply not seeing enough incremental signal yet. The mention trend is only slightly above trailing levels, so the story looks more like steady broadening than a fresh inflection; watch whether new capacity starts to loosen the bottleneck.

drivers.label: AI-driven demand acceleration
drivers.detail: Multiple reads describe advanced packaging spend, customer engagement, and content growth as being pulled by AI and high-performance compute.

drivers.label: Tight packaging capacity
drivers.detail: Companies repeatedly note constrained supply or tight capacity, implying continued pricing/power and backlog support for the ecosystem.

drivers.label: HBM and AI backend buildout
drivers.detail: HBM packaging expansion and backend growth are direct signals that advanced packaging demand is still outrunning installed capacity.

drivers.label: Revenue and outlook revisions higher
drivers.detail: Tools and process suppliers are seeing upside to revenue or outlook, showing demand is converting into near-term financial acceleration.

drivers.label: Technology complexity and chiplet adoption
drivers.detail: Rising 3D IC, chiplet, and advanced-package complexity supports higher test and process intensity.

risks: Advanced-packaging capacity additions could start easing the tightness faster than demand grows.
The mention trend is only 1% above trailing average, so incremental enthusiasm may be limited despite strong reads.
Exposure is uneven: several companies are neutral and may not convert the packaging trend into near-term upside.
